requirements.description: Projects must address next-generation AI agents, defined as autonomous systems powered by large AI language models (e.g., large language models or large multimodal models), capable of planning, utilizing tools and performing actions autonomously to achieve specified goals based on high-level instructions, where the large AI model acts as the agent's brain capable of interpreting instructions, generating plans, and using tools.

Research should address one or more of the following potential areas: (a) enhancing AI agent autonomy through advanced self-planning and self-optimization capabilities; (b) innovation in memory-augmented AI agents to facilitate robust long-term reasoning and lifelong learning; (c) developing advanced multi-agent frameworks tailored for collaborative agents, including AI agent frameworks based on mixed AI architectures; (d) advancing multimodal reasoning capabilities to enable real-world applications.

AI agent design must incorporate: (a) structured planning and reasoning methods to manage complex tasks, including robust planning, reasoning, and search mechanisms that allow agents to decompose tasks into structured subgoals; (b) effective memory and state management through balanced integration of short-term and external long-term memory solutions; (c) integration of external tools and APIs to overcome limitations in large AI models and enhance performance in accuracy-sensitive tasks; (d) appropriate validation and monitoring techniques.

To qualify for sector portfolio coverage at the award stage, projects should demonstrate application in one of three target sectors: healthcare, advanced manufacturing, or in-vehicle autonomous driving. At least one funded project per sector will be selected subject to passing all evaluation thresholds. Proposals not targeting these sectors remain eligible but may not benefit from the portfolio coverage provision.
Projects must link to the resources offered by the AI Factories, including the Data Labs.
All proposals must allocate tasks for cohesion activities with the European Partnership on AI, Data and Robotics (ADRA) and the CSA HORIZON-CL4-2025-03-HUMAN-18: GenAI4EU central Hub.
All proposals must incorporate mechanisms for assessing and demonstrating progress, including qualitative and quantitative KPIs, benchmarking, and progress monitoring.
Communicable results must be shared with the European R&D community through the AI-on-demand platform.
When possible, proposals should build on and reuse public results from relevant previous funded actions.
Proposals should build on or seek collaboration with relevant projects and develop synergies with other relevant international, European, national, or regional initiatives.
